commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=cc9d8d2d9aa68c259c2cc799ddfdbb88b0fd358c)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds `text_end`, `thinking_end`, `toolcall_start`, and `toolcall_end` to the opencode-go idle-timer progress predicate and adds a regression test for post-delta boundary-event liveness.

PR surface: Source +4, Tests +94. Total +98 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-reproducible. Current main forwards boundary events but only re-arms idle liveness on delta events, and the linked canonical issue includes a focused failing timing sequence; I did not execute tests because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Contributor action is needed for a code fix and real behavior proof; ClawSweeper should not take over an external PR while the proof gate is unmet.

target.title: fix(opencode-go): re-arm idle timer on block-boundary events to prevent false stalled-stream abort
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

When the opencode-go model finalizes a tool call and deliberates before the
next one, the provider emits real block-boundary SSE events
(`text_end`, `thinking_end`, `toolcall_start`, `toolcall_end`) that prove the
socket is alive. The stalled-stream watchdog only treated token deltas as
progress, so the idle timer could fire, abort a live stream, replace a
completed answer with a stalled error, and drop the provider's real `done`
event.

## Why This Change Was Made

`isProviderProgressEvent` now treats all four block-boundary events as
progress. `text_start` and `thinking_start` remain excluded because they are
synthetic preamble events and must not shorten the first-event window.

## User Impact

Live opencode-go answers are no longer falsely classified as stalled while
the provider is emitting valid block-boundary events between deltas.

## Evidence

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/opencode-go/stream-termination.test.ts`
  — 12 passed, including the boundary-event regression.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/llm-idle-timeout.test.ts`
  — 92 passed.
- `git diff --check` — clean.
- The regression drives the real stream wrapper with boundary events spaced
  below `idleTimeoutMs` and asserts that no false abort occurs.

Closes #96518
